title: Earnings call transcript: Airbnb Q4 2024 tops forecasts, shares surge
text: Airbnb delivered impressive fourth-quarter results, with earnings per share of $0.73 exceeding forecasts by nearly 24%. Revenue reached $2.5 billion, representing 12% year-over-year growth and surpassing analyst expectations. The market responded enthusiastically, with shares surging 14.52% in after-hours trading to $161.52. For the full year, the company generated $4.5 billion in free cash flow and repurchased $3.4 billion in stock. CEO Brian Chesky emphasized that 2025 marks the start of Airbnb's next chapter, signaling expansion beyond short-term rentals. The company plans to invest $200-$250 million launching new offerings, with products debuting in May 2025. The company expects Q1 2025 revenue between $2.23-$2.27 billion (4-6% growth) and maintains guidance for a full-year adjusted EBITDA margin of at least 34.5%. Management highlighted product improvements driving conversion gains and market share expansion, particularly in urban markets. The Cohost Network, launched four months prior, has grown to 100,000 listings with cohosts earning approximately double compared to standard listings.
